concept.conceptName: Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma

en.name: Lipase in blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context Pancreatic lipase is a digestive enzyme secreted by the exocrine pancreas that hydrolyses dietary triglycerides. Plasma lipase rises sharply within 4-8 hours of acute pancreatitis onset, peaks at 24-48 hours, and remains elevated for 8-14 days — longer than amylase. A serum lipase ≥ 3 × the upper limit of normal is one of three diagnostic criteria for acute pancreatitis (revised Atlanta classification) [1, 2]. Lipase is more sensitive and specific than amylase for pancreatic injury and has largely replaced it as the primary biomarker. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a biochemistry analyser using a colorimetric or turbidimetric method. Results are reported as an enzyme-activity concentration in U/L (international units per litre) or, in SI, in µkat/L [3]. ## Included Concepts The set lists one LOINC "Lab Test" concept (no LOINC Hierarchy is available in the loaded vocabulary): - *3004905 / 3040-3 — Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`U/L`** (concept 8645 — international unit per litre). Conversion: `1 U/L ≈ 0.01667 µkat/L`. Reference ranges are highly method-dependent (no international standard for lipase activity measurement); always preserve the upper limit of normal from the source lab. ## Excluded Concepts No concepts are explicitly excluded by name. The set deliberately omits: - **Lipase in 24-hour urine, amniotic fluid, body fluid, peritoneal fluid, pleural fluid, dialysis fluid specimens**: out of scope for a "blood" concept set. - **Sincalide challenge codes (`--Nth specimen post Sincalide`)**: challenge tests, out of scope. - **Cord blood and fetus specimens**: physiologically distinct from post-natal blood. ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a lipase value: `3004905 / 3040-3 — Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma`. - **Source reports lipase from ascitic fluid, peritoneal fluid, pleural fluid**: do **not** map. Out of scope. - **Do not confuse with**: amylase (separate concept set if defined, less specific for pancreatic injury), lipoprotein lipase (different enzyme, lipid metabolism). ## References [1] Banks PA, Bollen TL, Dervenis C, Gooszen HG, Johnson CD, Sarr MG, et al. Classification of acute pancreatitis—2012: revision of the Atlanta classification and definitions by international consensus. *Gut*. 2013;62(1):102-11. Available: https://doi.org/10.1136/gutjnl-2012-302779 [2] Working Group IAP/APA Acute Pancreatitis Guidelines. IAP/APA evidence-based guidelines for the management of acute pancreatitis. *Pancreatology*. 2013;13(4 Suppl 2):e1-15. Available: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.pan.2013.07.063 [3] Regenstrief Institute. LOINC code 3040-3 — Lipase [Enzymatic activity/volume] in Serum or Plasma. Available: https://loinc.org/3040-3/
